mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly compressing required returns and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. This mechanism operates continuously whenever rate cuts occur, independent of other macro conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.56% as of March 2008 reflects ongoing easing from prior tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism activates when unemployment rises above a critical threshold indicating cyclical weakness.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% in March 2008, near cyclical lows but beginning to inflect upward
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth and housing starts jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously, it indicates broad-based demand destruction that reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative equity returns. The interaction captures demand-side recession risk better than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1575k units in February 2008, down sharply from prior year; RSAFS at 683974M in March 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% MoM, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and supply-side health. Sustained declines in INDPRO signal supply-side weakness and reduced productive capacity, which constrains earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.55 in February 2008, showing early signs of weakness in manufacturing sector
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO declines 2 consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth directly reflects labor demand and income generation capacity. Declining payroll levels reduce aggregate household income and consumer spending, which depresses corporate revenues and equity valuations. This mechanism operates as a continuous trend signal without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153018k in March 2008, beginning to show employment weakness from financial crisis
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI and monetary policy stance measured by FEDFUNDS interact to determine real interest rates and equity risk premiums. When inflation rises while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates compress equity valuations. When inflation falls with FEDFUNDS declining, real rates support equity valuations. The interaction captures the joint effect on discount rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 0.5pp while CPIAUCSL growth slows to < 0.3% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 296.76 in March 2008; FEDFUNDS at 2.56% reflecting aggressive easing
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly captures aggregate demand strength. Declining or negative GDP growth signals recession and produces negative equity returns as corporate earnings expectations fall. This mechanism is regime-conditional: it is strongest in late-cycle expansions where growth deceleration signals imminent contraction.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19895.27B in Q4 2007, with growth decelerating into 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production and payroll employment jointly measure supply-side capacity and labor utilization. When both decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based supply-side contraction and reduced productive capacity, which constrains earnings growth and produces negative equity returns. The interaction captures supply-shock risk better than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series showing early weakness in early 2008 from financial crisis spillovers
regime_hint: supply-side contraction with labor market weakness
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline MoM.
